text: Abstract This paper explores how the quality of the base liquor affects the final liquor quality and potential health risk of soy sauce-aroma type baijiu (SSAB). And how different quality grades of base liquor affect the SSAB through their composition. The sauce, floral and fruity, fermented aromas and fermented taste indicators like softness, fullness, harmony, purity and persistence of SSAB are positively correlated with the quality grade of the base liquor. The electronic nose (E-nose) is able to differentiate between base liquor of different quality grades. Through the untargeted metabolome analysis by HS-SPME-GC-MS, 16 differential compounds are detected in all samples from different fermentation rounds, including 11 esters, 1 alcohol, 2 aldehydes and 2 ketones. According to the contents of aromatics, alcohols, aldehydes and ketones in the base liquor, the higher the quality grade, the richer the content of them. Through principal component analysis (PCA) biplots of the differential compounds in different quality grades of base liquor, the fruity aroma is found to be stronger in the superior-grade base liquor. And by correlation analysis of differential compounds and the sensors responses of E-nose, it is shown that furfuryl ethyl ether, butanoic acid ethyl ester, isopentyl hexanoate, nonanoic acid ethyl ester and 3-methyl-1-butanol have significant effect on the response intensity of E-nose sensors.
